lines: # Default is 100. If set to 0, the size check is disabled.
+# Maximum file size in bytes for files read from or written to a provisioning repository
+# through the files API. Applies symmetrically to GET (reads) and POST/PUT writes.
+# Default is 5242880 bytes (5 MiB, i.e. 5 * 1024 * 1024). Set to 0 (or any
# Public-facing root URL of this Grafana instance, used by provisioning to construct URLs
# that must be reachable from external systems (e.g. GitHub PR-comment image fetchers and
# GitHub webhook deliveries). When empty, falls back to [server] root_url. Set this when
-	// Files endpoint max size for dashboards etc (5MB)
+	// maxFileSize caps the size in bytes of files read from or written to the
+	// repository through this connector. <=0 disables the check.

-func NewFilesConnector(getter RepoGetter, parsers resources.ParserFactory, clients resources.ClientFactory, access auth.AccessChecker, folderMetadataEnabled bool, folderAPIVersion string) *filesConnector {

+func NewFilesConnector(getter RepoGetter, parsers resources.ParserFactory, clients resources.ClientFactory, access auth.AccessChecker, folderMetadataEnabled bool, folderAPIVersion string, maxFileSize int64) *filesConnector {
@@ -45,7 +43,39 @@ func NewFilesConnector(getter RepoGetter, parsers resources.ParserFactory, clien
+// sizeLimitedReaderWriter wraps a repository.ReaderWriter and rejects reads
+// that exceed maxBytes. The check fires immediately after the underlying
+// repository returns the file bytes, so callers (including DualReadWriter
+// which parses the result) never see oversized payloads.
+func (s *sizeLimitedReaderWriter) Read(ctx context.Context, path, ref string) (*repository.FileInfo, error) {
+	if s.maxBytes > 0 && info != nil && int64(len(info.Data)) > s.maxBytes {
+			fmt.Sprintf("file %q is %d bytes; max allowed is %d bytes", info.Path, len(info.Data), s.maxBytes),
+// withSizeLimit returns rw wrapped so its Read method enforces the connector's
+// configured max file size. Returns rw unchanged when the cap is disabled.
@@ -105,6 +135,10 @@ func (c *filesConnector) handleRequest(ctx context.Context, name string, r *http
+	// Enforce max_file_size right at the repo boundary so oversized payloads
+	// are rejected before parsing/DryRun runs in DualReadWriter.Read or before
+	// the bytes are streamed back from handleGetRawFile.
dualReadWriter, authorizer, err := c.createDualReadWriter(ctx, repo, readWriter)
@@ -310,7 +344,7 @@ func (c *filesConnector) handlePost(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request, opts r
@@ -326,7 +360,7 @@ func (c *filesConnector) handlePost(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request, opts r

func (c *filesConnector) handleMove(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request, opts resources.DualWriteOptions, isDir bool, dualReadWriter *resources.DualReadWriter) (*provisioning.ResourceWrapper, error) {
// For move operations, only read body for file moves (not directory moves)
@@ -348,7 +382,7 @@ func (c *filesConnector) handlePut(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request, opts re
@@ -362,7 +396,7 @@ func (c *filesConnector) handlePut(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request, opts re

func (c *filesConnector) handleFolderMetadataUpdate(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request, opts resources.DualWriteOptions, dualReadWriter *resources.DualReadWriter) (*provisioning.ResourceWrapper, error) {
+			// handleRequest wraps readWriter with the size limiter at the
+			// connector boundary. Mirror that here so the unit test exercises
+			// the same enforcement point as production.
+		// Default cap for the files API. Callers (e.g. RegisterAPIService)
+		// may overwrite b.maxFileSize after construction; any non-positive
// Return the preferred (v0alpha1) builder since it runs controllers/workers
// level operations remain Admin-gated by authorizeRepositorySubresource.
-	storage[provisioning.RepositoryResourceInfo.StoragePath("files")] = NewFilesConnector(b, b.parsers, b.clients, filesAccess, b.folderMetadataEnabled, b.folderAPIVersion)
+	storage[provisioning.RepositoryResourceInfo.StoragePath("files")] = NewFilesConnector(b, b.parsers, b.clients, filesAccess, b.folderMetadataEnabled, b.folderAPIVersion, b.maxFileSize)
-// readBody reads the request body and limits the size
+// readBody reads the request body and limits the size. A non-positive
+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading request body: %w", err)
-			return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: max size %d bytes", errMsgRequestTooLarge, maxSize)
+				fmt.Sprintf("%s: max size %d bytes", errMsgRequestTooLarge, maxSize),
return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading request body: %w", err)
+			body:    "anything goes when the cap is unlimited",
+			body:    "anything goes when the cap is unlimited",
+// ProvisioningMaxFileSizeDefault is the default value for the
+// [provisioning] max_file_size key (5 MiB). It bounds files read from or
+// written to a provisioning repository through the files API.
ProvisioningMaxRepositories               int64         // default 10, 0 in config = unlimited (converted to -1 internally)
ProvisioningMaxIncrementalChanges         int           // default 100, 0 in config = unlimited
+	ProvisioningMaxFileSize                   int64         // bytes; default 5 MiB (5242880); <=0 = unlimited
ProvisioningPublicRootURL                 string        // public-facing root URL of this Grafana instance for provisioning consumers (webhooks, screenshots); falls back to AppURL when empty
+// WithProvisioningMaxFileSize overrides the per-file size cap enforced by the
+// files API on both reads and writes. A small value (e.g. 1024) keeps the
+// fixture cheap to generate; any non-positive value (<=0) disables the check
+// entirely. Pass an int64 — the helper takes its address so GrafanaOpts can
// WithoutExportFeatureFlag disables the provisioningExport feature flag.
// Remove provisioningExport from the enabled feature toggles
+// enforcement of [provisioning] max_file_size on raw files (e.g. README.md).
+// A file under the configured cap is served as-is; a file over the cap is
+// rejected with HTTP 413 Request Entity Too Large.
+	t.Run("GET small raw file under limit succeeds", func(t *testing.T) {
+		require.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, resp.StatusCode, "small README should be served")
+			"under-limit raw file should be served verbatim")
+	t.Run("GET raw file over limit returns 413", func(t *testing.T) {
+		require.Equal(t, http.StatusRequestEntityTooLarge, resp.StatusCode, "oversized README should be rejected with 413")
+		require.Contains(t, string(body), "max allowed", "error body should advertise the cap")
+// enforcement: a POST whose body exceeds [provisioning] max_file_size is
+// rejected before the resource is parsed or persisted.
+	// A minimal but well-formed dashboard JSON, padded to exceed the cap.
+	// Padding lives inside the title field so the body is still valid JSON.
+	require.Greater(t, len(oversized), int(testMaxFileSize), "fixture must exceed the configured cap")
+	require.Error(t, result.Error(), "oversized POST should be rejected")
+		"oversized POST should return HTTP 413; got %v", result.Error())
+		"error should advertise the size cap; got %v", result.Error())
+// The "0 = unlimited" semantics are covered by the unit test
+// not exercise it as a separate integration test here: spinning up a second
+// Grafana inside this package would share the package-wide test database
+// with the shared env, and the shared env's controllers would then attempt
+// to validate the second server's repos against their own (different)
+// testMaxFileSize is the per-file cap installed in the shared test server.
+// Small enough that fixtures stay cheap; large enough that ordinary
+// fixtures (text-options.json etc.) still fit comfortably under it.
@@ -800,6 +800,14 @@ func createGrafDir(t *testing.T, tmpDir string, opts GrafanaOpts) (string, strin
+	// nil means "use the ini default" (5 MB). Non-nil writes the value,
+	// including 0 which disables the per-file size check.
